messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That's the kind of thing that's easy to brush off at first, but it sounds like it's been on your mind if you're bringing it up now. Has it changed at all since you first noticed it, or is it still the same?

And - have you had a chance to get it checked out, or are you still in the "should I bother?" phase?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

I can understand why that would start to weigh on you a bit. A few weeks, not going away, maybe getting slightly bigger - that's the kind of thing that deserves a proper look from a doctor rather than just waiting it out.

The good news is that lumps under the arm are really common and most of the time they turn out to be something pretty ordinary - a swollen lymph node from a minor infection, a cyst, that kind of thing. But the firmness and the fact that it's persisting does mean it's worth getting checked rather than hoping it disappears.

Have you got a doctor you can see fairly easily? I'd genuinely encourage you to make an appointment soon - not in a panic, but just don't keep putting it off either. A week or two to get seen would be reasonable.

How are you feeling about it all?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That makes total sense - a lot of people feel that way about doctors, and it's really easy to convince yourself it's nothing when the alternative feels stressful.

But I want to be honest with you, because I think you kind of already know this: a lump that's firm, not painful, and has been growing for a couple of weeks is exactly the kind of thing doctors say you shouldn't sit on. That combination of features is worth ruling out properly, not just hoping for the best.

The "I hate doctors" + "I'm sure it's fine" thing is a pretty understandable way to avoid anxiety in the short term - but it sounds like it's not actually working, since you're thinking about it enough to mention it tonight.

What is it about doctors that you hate? Is it the waiting, the worry about what they might find, something else? Sometimes it helps to just name what the actual barrier is.
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

Honestly? That combination of features - firm, painless, slowly growing - is the pattern that doctors take seriously and want to investigate promptly. It's not the typical presentation of something like a reactive lymph node from an infection, which tends to be softer, tender, and clears up in a week or two.

That doesn't mean it's definitely something serious. There are benign things that can present this way. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I said "yeah probably fine, no rush."

The honest read is: this needs to be seen soon, not eventually. Like, this week if you can.

I know that's probably not what the part of you that was hoping for reassurance wanted to hear. But I think you asked because you already sensed the answer, and you deserve a straight response rather than me just making you feel better in the moment.

Can you make yourself book something tomorrow?
